dialogues.text_en: Twelve have mercy...
We did everything we could. Everything... But it simply wasn't enough...
I can scarcely believe my eyes...
How could this happen?
Father, no! This cannot be!
Will you be returning to Sunperch?
How... How could this happen?

dialogues.character: Vow of Resolve Gulool Ja Ja
dialogues.text_en: Enough... I am beyond saving...
Father, please! You can't give up!

dialogues.character: Vow of Resolve Gulool Ja Ja
dialogues.text_en: Are you there...old friend...?
I'm right here. I'm right here.

dialogues.character: Vow of Resolve Gulool Ja Ja
dialogues.text_en: Look after...my children...
What?
I'm an old fossil like you. I've only got so many years left.
But if that's what you want, it would be an honor.

dialogues.character: Vow of Resolve Gulool Ja Ja
dialogues.text_en: You and I...must go on another journey together someday...
Aye, ye can count on it.

dialogues.character: Vow of Resolve Gulool Ja Ja
dialogues.text_en: Wuk Lamat... Koana...
Yes, Papa.

dialogues.character: Vow of Resolve Gulool Ja Ja
dialogues.text_en: The graves of the Yok Huy... You saw them, yes? I always found their teachings comforting...
Please, Father, you must save your strength.

dialogues.character: Vow of Resolve Gulool Ja Ja
dialogues.text_en: Our flesh may wither and die. But so long as we are remembered, we are never truly gone.

dialogues.character: Vow of Resolve Gulool Ja Ja
dialogues.text_en: Through you, my hopes survive... Through you, I live on...

dialogues.character: Vow of Resolve Gulool Ja Ja
dialogues.text_en: So do not mourn me. Walk tall...and see our nation to a brighter future.
Papa! Papa!
Papa!!!
What can we do now...?
Gulool Ja Ja was so proud to be succeeded by his children. To think that he will never get to see them carry on his legacy...
At a time like this, we must take the initiative. There will be countless people in need of help.
I don't understand...
If only I'd been more careful...
We must see to the people... Assess casualties, as well as any damage done to the city. Yes...
R-Right. So, uh... I...
Vow Wuk Lamat. Vow Koana. I bring word from the city. The enemy has withdrawn, and the Landsguard have begun rendering aid to the people.
Casualties within our ranks have hindered progress, but we continue to do all that we can.
I thank you for the report. Please inform the guard they are to prioritize such efforts until further notice.
The people... I need to help them.
We will assist you in whatever way we can, of course.
Thank you, thank you. There are sure to be wounded everywhere─more than I could count.
We'll split up and help all of them. You can count on us!
Alphinaud and Alisaie, could you take Bayside Bevy? And Krile and Erenville, the palace perimeter.
As for Forename , please come to the Resplendent Quarter with me. We'll all meet back here afterwards.
We will close off Sunperch for now. We need to ascertain the full extent of the situation.
Don't you worry about Koana. I'll keep an eye on the lad.
How could Zoraal Ja commit so heinous an act!?
If you're still in pain, there's no need to push yourself.
You should be all right now.
I'm afraid we were too late to save him...
Someone tell those enemy soldiers to throw down their weapons and grab a taco instead. There's no conflict that can't be resolved over a good meal!
I'm not entirely sure I understand what just happened, but seeing as I made it through unscathed, I'm going to keep doing what needs to be done.
I'm glad some of the crates survived the attack. We haven't lost everything... We can still recover.
Even in times of war, the Wing of Reflection has a role to play. Our most pressing job is ensuring the Vow of Resolve can concentrate on the fight ahead.
In response to the attack, we have stepped up patrols and bolstered security. You and your compatriots, however, will always be welcome in the palace.
So Bakool Ja Ja protected you... That's wonderful.
This isn't how I'd hoped to celebrate the crowning of our new Dawnservants. Hopefully it's resolved quickly...
Those...machines that attacked us─where did they come from? Is this city even safe anymore?
The founding of our great nation was supposed to put an end to the war and bloodshed! We don't know how to fight─we weren't trained for this!
Welcome back, sir. Will you be making use of your cabin? We have been fortunate to escape any serious damage from the invasion...
We've scarcely had time to welcome our new rulers, and now this? Can't we have a day of peace in this damned country?
Where are you hurt?
Why has it gotten so quiet out there? I'm not sure I want to find out... No, I think what I need right now is a warm, soothing bath...
Why did this happen...?
No matter what's floating up there, we'll get you to Yak T'el safely.
Looks like we'll have to replace the whole pillar. It was only a glancing blow, but the force of the explosion must have bent the damn thing...
I'm sorry I raised my voice. It's just...I don't know what to do...
I've gathered up all the unbroken crates I could find. Some are a little burnt around the edges, but hopefully their contents are all right.
Th-Thanks... I think I'm alright...
Did you see them? Those soldiers?
They looked like people, but they killed and killed with no hesitation...
If they attack us again, how can we hope to defend ourselves...?
<sniffle> Thank you, sir / miss .
B-But my husband and son... They were...they were... <sob>
My thanks. I'll be fine now.
But we failed to protect the people...
Why did so many have to die? Where were Vow Wuk Lamat and Vow Koana!?
Ugh, they're not to blame. I understand, I do, but... <sigh> I need to clear my head...
Thank you... I thought I was done for...
I heard something disturbing. The enemy leader─they say he looked like Zoraal Ja.
But that can't be true, can it? Why would the First Promise attack his own people?
But if it was him, what happens now? Is he going to come back? What do we do if he does?
Th-Thank you, the pain is better now. But I can't stop shaking...
Where did they come from? Why did they attack us? And why are those things still in the sky?
We had peace under Gulool Ja Ja! We were safe! But now...
You've finished aiding the people here?
Thank you.
There were children among the wounded. Children .
...No, now isn't the time. There are still others who need help.
Could you go and see how things are at Talonmarch? And afterwards, go and find Alisaie at Bayside Bevy.
The people are shaken. We must do what we can to help them, and quickly.
It breaks my heart to see what's become of Tuliyollal. I can't begin to imagine how the people are feeling.
I've helped all those I could find, and seen them delivered into the Landsguard's care.
As many casualties as there are, it would've been worse if the Landsguard hadn't fought as bravely as they did.
And if Zoraal Ja actually meant to harm the people...
I mean, look around. The buildings are hardly damaged.
I suspect his aim was more to incite fear. To show that he could strike whenever he pleases.
...But we can discuss that later. How is Lamaty'i?
Even after what happened to her father, she's trying so hard to fulfill her duty to the people...
Still, everyone has their limits. She needs rest, and soon.
Someone mentioned seeing her head towards the shore. Perhaps she's gone to the Gornitruq Gleamsands.
Steady, now!
I've brought you medicine. Drink!
There you are, Lamaty'i! I was beginning to─
Come on. Come on, take it. Please!
Lamaty'i...
Lamaty'i!
How...? How could I...?
Look at me, Lamaty'i.
You need rest, you and him / her both. I will tend to this soul.
But my people... I have to help them...
The Landsguard have matters well in hand. Trust in them.
If there's another attack, your people will need you to protect them. They'll need you to be sharp.
So please, rest. Consider it your duty.
I stood before them all... Said that we'd build a nation where everyone knows happiness...
So much for that promise!
What good is a Vow of Resolve who cannot save her people!?
You'll stick with me?
You're right. This isn't the time for sulking.
Thank you. My head's a little clearer now. I need to concentrate on what I can do rather than what I can't.
We've dealt with unfriendly factions before. From Yok Huy still longing for conquest to adherents of blessed siblings─people who were unwilling to listen.
Even so, I believed that by learning about one another and engaging in good faith, we could peacefully settle our differences. And we did.
But my brother is different. There'll be no reasoning with him. He killed our people─our father. He'll stop at nothing to achieve his goal.
What's more, he has power. The power to destroy the peace we've worked so hard to preserve.
If I'm to protect that peace, then I must fight...
I must confront Zoraal Ja─and kill him!
I'm fine. Just needed a moment to settle my nerves.
Thank you for being there for me, and for urging me on despite all my sulking.
Sitting around complaining isn't very leader-like, is it?
So much has happened so quickly. I struggle to make sense of it all.
The strange soldiers, those floating ships, the new power Zoraal Ja commands...
Given everything he's done, and still intends to do, I know I can't afford to run headlong into battle. Not this time.
I cannot let this terrible pain in my heart lead me astray. But I will make it my strength─the strength to do what I must.
Right, I'm heading back to the palace. I need to talk strategy with Koana.
Could you and the others join us in a while? However we decide to proceed, I want you all to be aware of it.
Oh, and...could you tell Alisaie I'll be all right? She's probably at Bayside Bevy.
Oh, you're back. By that, I take it to mean Lamaty'i is feeling better.
She needs to be strong for the people of Tural, I understand that. But she doesn't have to do it alone.
So if she intends to fight, then we fight. Together.
That said...
I wish I knew exactly what we were up against.
